title: Ability to use x86_64 Fedora CoreOS image as a base on M1 ARM Macs
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

It would be good if there was the ability to specify `podman machine init` to use an x86_64 image as the machine's VM image rather than using the `aarch64` image which is what it currently uses. Setting a specific image with `--image-path` allows you to set an x86_64 image however podman still sets all of the QEMU options in `~/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/machine.json` as if it was an `aarch64` image.

The reason this would be useful is some container images have issues running on the `aarch64` vm with `qemu-x86_64-static` emulator. I've been able to get this to work manually by changing the `CmdLine` section of `machine.json` to the following, using `qemu 6.2.0_1` 

```json
"CmdLine": [
  "/opt/homebrew/bin/qemu-system-x86_64",
  "-m",
  "2048",
  "-smp",
  "1",
  "-fw_cfg",
  "name=opt/com.coreos/config,file=/Users/pierswalter/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/intel-new.ign",
  "-qmp",
  "unix://var/folders/vj/ycgbg5p54kd46bkpz2b8nxcm0000gn/T/podman/qmp_intel-new.sock,server=on,wait=off",
  "-netdev",
  "socket,id=vlan,fd=3",
  "-device",
  "virtio-net-pci,netdev=vlan,mac=5a:94:ef:e4:0c:ee",
  "-device",
  "virtio-serial",
  "-chardev",
  "socket,path=/var/folders/vj/ycgbg5p54kd46bkpz2b8nxcm0000gn/T/podman/intel-new_ready.sock,server=on,wait=off,id=intel-new_ready",
  "-device",
  "virtserialport,chardev=intel-new_ready,name=org.fedoraproject.port.0",
  "-drive",
  "file=/opt/homebrew/share/qemu/edk2-x86_64-code.fd,if=pflash,format=raw,readonly=on",
  "-virtfs",
  "local,path=/Users/pierswalter,mount_tag=vol0,security_model=mapped-xattr",
  "-drive",
  "if=virtio,file=/Users/pierswalter/.local/share/containers/podman/machine/qemu/intel-new_fedora-coreos-36.20220703.3.1-qemu.x86_64.qcow2"
 ],
```
The underlying VM does run slower but provides better compatibility with container images which require the x86_64 architecture. 
A command such as `podman machine init --x86_64` would be great for M1 macs to run an x86_64 VM rather than the `aarch64` one. This could in future allow other architectures to be the VM host as well if desired

comments.body:
There was some work here (https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/12430) but it got never finished.

We recommend to use qemu-user-static instead so that you only have to emulate the container application and not the full OS.
If we move to native Apple virtualization, on M! machines they have supposedly done some enhancements that make running an X86 ARCH more attractive.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@baude continues to work on this, and it is getting very close.

Hey guys! 

Is there any chance of supporting apple virtualization in the near future like colima does?

Thanks.
Yes we hope to have tech preview in 4.7
@rhatdan Hey! Can we expect support in 4.7? Thanks.
Closing, as we now use AppleVirtualization with Rosetta support.
